channel.title: Life Lens System — Journal
channel.description: The build journal of the Life Lens System. Context, implications, and the story behind each discovery.

item.title: Weak Signals, Strong Patterns
item.description: What LinkedIn reactions reveal about your network — if you listen. How the Life Lens System picks up public signals, cross-references them against a personal database of 21,000+ persons, and classifies them into rings of relevance. Demonstrated with real data from a single LinkedIn post.

item.title: Cluster Firing
item.description: When a city center becomes a neural network. How myelin, saltatory conduction and action potentials work together in the Life Lens System, demonstrated with real data from 29 shops in Haarlem.

item.title: Fifty Brains on a Laptop
item.description: Once your Life Lens System has a brain, it can multiply fifty times. Ten Tom sessions, five sub-agents each, fifty parallel thinkers on a laptop. But fifty times nothing is nothing. The multiplication only works because the foundation is solid.
